proven.claim: PROVEN: null guard ensures inferType's null return never contaminates the types map.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 307
; PROVEN: null guard ensures inferType's null return never contaminates the types map.
; Frame: transition + negation of property → expect unsat.

(declare-const infer_returned_null Int)  ; 1 = inferType returned null
(declare-const types_updated Int)        ; 1 = types[nameNode.text] was assigned

; Code transition: inferType returns null for unrecognized value node types
; Code transition: if (inferred) — falsy values (null) skip the assignment
(assert (=> (= infer_returned_null 1) (= types_updated 0)))

; Negation of property: types is updated even when inferType returned null
(assert (= infer_returned_null 1))
(assert (= types_updated 1))

(check-sat)

violations.claim: REACHABLE: `const s = "prefix_" + id` → inferType returns "number", not "string".
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 307
; REACHABLE: `const s = "prefix_" + id` → inferType returns "number", not "string".
; Downstream callers of extractLocalTypes receive a semantically wrong type annotation.

(declare-const has_plus_op Int)          ; 1 = binary_expression has a + child node
(declare-const left_is_string_lit Int)   ; 1 = left operand is a string literal
(declare-const recorded_type Int)        ; 0 = "number" recorded, 1 = "string" recorded
(declare-const actual_type Int)          ; 0 = number, 1 = string

; Code transition: binary_expression case in inferType
;   op = valueNode.children.find(c => c.type === "+") — found
(assert (= has_plus_op 1))

; Code transition: if (op) return "number" — operator found → records "number"
(assert (=> (= has_plus_op 1) (= recorded_type 0)))

; Code transition: left operand is a string literal → actual result type is string
(assert (= left_is_string_lit 1))
(assert (=> (= left_is_string_lit 1) (= actual_type 1)))

; Violation: recorded type (number) differs from actual type (string)
(assert (= recorded_type 0))
(assert (= actual_type 1))

(check-sat)
